intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Knavel." It's possible that it's a misspelling or a lesser-known plant. However, I can provide information on the plant Knave's Thyme, also known as Creeping Thyme (Thymus serpyllum) or Knave's Jelly, although I couldn't confirm the Knavel term.

If we consider Knave's Thyme, it's a low-growing, spreading herb with tiny leaves, belonging to the mint family. The health benefits of Knave's Thyme include its antimicrobial, antifungal, and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate coughs and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Knave's Thyme include volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Knave's Thyme include teas, infusions, and topical creams for skin and wound care, as well as essential oils for aromatherapy and inhalation.
